message:
fix(encoding): validate config before empty-payload fast path to prevent silent misconfiguration

EncodingPipeline::plan_blocks previously returned an empty block list
for empty payloads without checking whether the config itself was valid
(e.g., symbol_size=0, NaN repair_overhead, pool/config symbol size
mismatch). This meant that encoding an empty payload with an invalid
config would silently succeed with zero symbols, while encoding a
non-empty payload with the same config would correctly fail.

The fix extracts config validation into a separate validate_config
method that is called at the top of plan_blocks before the empty-data
early return. This ensures invalid configs fail closed regardless of
payload size.

Tests added:
- empty_payload_still_rejects_zero_symbol_size
- empty_payload_still_rejects_invalid_repair_overhead
- empty_payload_still_rejects_pool_symbol_size_mismatch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
